articles.title: Blue Origin seeks to resume New Glenn launches by year’s end
articles.summary:
Blue Origin’s CEO says damage to its New Glenn launch pad is not as bad as feared and the rocket could return to flight by the end of the year.
The post Blue Origin seeks to resume New Glenn launches by year’s end appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: How long will it take to rebuild Blue Origin's launch pad? We asked some SpaceX vets.
articles.summary: "Everyone is in a place where it’s no fun to be there."

articles.title: SSC Space Launches Red Kite-Powered Sounding Rocket Carrying 12 Experiments
articles.summary:
SSC Space launched its SubOrbital Express-5 mission on 31 May from the Esrange Space Centre in northern Sweden. The rocket carried 12 individual experiments, one of which examined the effects of microgravity on red hair samples from a Swedish influencer. The 13-metre-tall sounding rocket used for the mission was powered by Red Kite rocket motors […]
The post SSC Space Launches Red Kite-Powered Sounding Rocket Carrying 12 Experiments appeared first on European Spaceflight.

articles.title: Typhoon Jangmi
articles.summary: The sprawling storm promised to deliver torrential rain across a wide swath of southern Japan.

articles.title: Live coverage: SpaceX to launch 24 Starlink satellites on Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg
articles.summary: The Starlink 17-47 mission will see SpaceX attempt its 200th landing on the drone ship called ‘Of Course I Still Love You.’ Liftoff from pad 4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base is scheduled for 7:36 a.m. PDT (10:36 a.m. EDT / 1436 UTC).

articles.title: Blue Origin vows to resume New Glenn flights by year’s end
articles.summary: Blue Origin CEO Dave Limp, in an overnight post on the social media platform X, said propellant tanks  at launch pad 36 at the Cape Canaveral Space Force Station made it through the blast in good shape, as did a nearby processing hangar. The main support gantry, while damaged, can be repaired in place.

articles.title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
articles.summary:
Blue Origin has wasted no time launching inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36…
The post Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026 appeared first on NASASpaceFlight.com.

articles.title: Live coverage: SpaceX to launch 29 Starlink satellites on Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral
articles.summary: The Starlink 10-43 mission will fly on a Falcon 9 rocket heading on a north-easterly trajectory. Liftoff from pad 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station is scheduled for 6:11 a.m. EDT (1011 UTC).

articles.title: NASA Sets Launch Date for Roman Space Telescope

articles.title: NRO nominee says commercial space, AI are reshaping spy satellite agency
articles.summary:
In a confirmation hearing, Roger Mason said clear government demand signals are needed as private investment transforms the space intelligence market

articles.title: NASA Space Roboticist Challenge
articles.summary: The Fly Foundational Robots (FFR) mission will launch a robotic arm, with seven degrees of freedom, to low Earth orbit. NASA is opening access to the robotic arm to a select group of U.S. researchers — principal investigators, post-doctoral researchers, professors, and highly qualified graduate students — who have a compelling experiment and the capability […]

articles.title: Look Up!
articles.summary: Astronauts Sophie Adenot of ESA (European Space Agency) and Jack Hathaway of NASA, both Expedition 74 flight engineers, look out a window in the cupola, monitoring the automated approach and docking of the SpaceX Dragon cargo spacecraft to the International Space Station on May 17, 2026. The orbital outpost was soaring 259 miles above the […]
